So on production-shaped logs Sol's fold never sees a terminal: child.working goes True on the first submit and stays True forever, decide() converts every later plan to REPRICE (sol_policy.py:882-883), and the incumbent seam refuses it — STAGE_REFUSED_STALE 'REPRICE planned but no live working order with a venue id' (app/store/core.py:2946-2960). The refusal event does not clear the fold, so it is a permanent per-tick livelock: every post-fill protective exit is refused_stale forever, and tranche second legs are never even planned (child.working blocks the branch at sol_policy.py:827). Functionally identical to REV-0023 F4 at our seam (incumbent fix: app/sellside/policy.py:135-158 scans UNFILTERED history for terminals). Sol's suite masks it by constructing venue events WITH envelope_id=env.id (test_sol_policy.py:251-258, 341-348, 366-384) — a shape the production log never emits — and the MANIFEST conformance selection excludes tests/test_wo0025_multileg.py (whose _order_event at :108-117 uses the realistic shape). _tranche_filled has the same filter defect: it returned False in the experiment despite a deduped 50-share FILL in history.

verdicts.evidence:
work/collab/SOL-0001/impl/sol_policy.py:260-327 (_child_state) and :743,780-894 (decide). In _child_state, the "reprice" branch (lines 294-295) is:
    elif action == "reprice":
        anonymous_working = anonymous_working or not working_ids
This never adds the reprice event's own order_id (the newly-minted child, e.g. ord-2) into working_ids, and only touches anonymous_working when working_ids was already empty. So a submit(ord-1)->reprice(ord-2)->CANCELED(ord-1) sequence: (1) submit sets working_ids={ord-1}; (2) reprice sees working_ids non-empty so anonymous_working stays False and ord-2 is never recorded anywhere; (3) the CANCELED terminal-event branch (lines 305-311) does working_ids.discard("ord-1") since event.order_id is not None, leaving working_ids={} and anonymous_working still False. Net: child.working = bool(working_ids) or anonymous_working = False, even though ord-2 is live.
In decide(), the crash/stop-triggered branch (lines 794-823) unconditionally builds a PlannedAction with kind=ActionKind.SUBMIT (no child.working check at all); the only place SUBMIT is ever upgraded to REPRICE is line 882-883: `if child.working: desired = PlannedAction(**{**desired.__dict__, "kind": ActionKind.REPRICE})`. Since child.working is False (per the fold bug above), desired.kind remains SUBMIT despite ord-2 resting live. No test in test_sol_policy.py exercises a submit(order_id)->reprice->CANCELED(same id) sequence (grep for "reprice" only finds order-id-less reprice sequences at lines 399-400 and an unrelated urgency test at line 302), so this defect is untested and unremediated in Sol's suite.
verdicts.reason: Read the exact code path the finding names (_child_state fold and the decide() kind-selection/upgrade logic in work/collab/SOL-0001/impl/sol_policy.py). The finding is accurate: Sol's reprice branch in _child_state never registers the reprice-minted child's order_id into working_ids/anonymous_working, so once the original submit's order_id is later marked CANCELED, the fold's `working` flag collapses to False even though the repriced child order is still live. Since decide()'s only SUBMIT->REPRICE upgrade path (line 882-883) gates strictly on child.working, and the stop-triggered/crash branch (lines 794-823) always constructs kind=SUBMIT first, this produces exactly the described bug: decide() emits a fresh SUBMIT plan on top of a live child order after a routine submit->reprice->CANCELED(original id) lifecycle. This is a genuine defect in Sol's child-state fold / kind-selection, not tested anywhere in test_sol_policy.py.
